rows.notes: Amounts for pounds (336) and shillings (2) agreed by both extractors. For pence, gemini-flash read 3 pence with an external '4' as an annotation, while gpt-5-mini read 3 pence and 0.75 fraction from a 'small stroke'. Visual verification confirms '3' pence is clearly within the column. The subsequent '4' is physically distinct and positioned outside the pence column's typical bounds, making it an annotation or extraneous mark rather than part of the valid pence amount or a standard fraction symbol for 0.75. '34' pence is an invalid amount (violates pence < 12 rule). Therefore, gemini-flash's interpretation of 3 pence with the '4' as an annotation is selected. Description from gemini-flash is also more accurate with the period.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to J. St. Brickland for a buttery/butt book, amounting to £0 11s 6d.

rows.description: The same to Mr. Belfield by the Consent of the Society Befield.
rows.notes: Pounds (0), shillings (19), and pence (9) agreed by both extractors. Fractions differed (gemini-flash: 0.25, gpt-5-mini: 0.5). Visual verification clearly shows a 'q' character after the '9' in pence, which consistently denotes a farthing (0.25). This aligns with gemini-flash. Description from gemini-flash is also a better match ('Befield.' vs 'B. Belfield').
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr. Belfield (by consent of the Society) for the same purpose as the previous commons entry.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr. Belfield for omitted commons (by consent of the Society), amounting to £0 19s 9¼d.
